event_text: For the week ending 2026-02-06, Lower 48 natural gas storage posted a unusually large drawdown of 252 Bcf, leaving inventories at 2202 Bcf. Storage stood 95 Bcf below the level a year earlier and 706 Bcf below the rolling 52-week average. This suggests tighter near-term supply conditions in the natural gas market.

For the week ending 2026-01-30, Lower 48 natural gas storage posted a unusually large drawdown of 359 Bcf, leaving inventories at 2454 Bcf. Storage stood 57 Bcf above the level a year earlier and 455 Bcf below the rolling 52-week average. This suggests tighter near-term supply conditions in the natural gas market. This crossed the GPD Value-at-Risk tail threshold.
